+

+

+14. `rg` (via Grep tool, `-n`) for `submit_action` in `crates/editor-shell/src` and in `editor/rge-editor/src`. Result: only the shell-side definition + module doc comments; **no** external production caller.

Confirmed the keyboard input branch dispatches only to `handle_key_command` (which reaches `undo`/`redo`/`mark_saved`), and that `playback.rs` is explicitly bus-free.
+16. `rg` (via Grep tool, with `Glob`-mode search) for `CommandBus|submit_action|command_bus|undo_command|redo_command|mark_saved_command|handle_key_command` to enumerate files. Confirmed the production user-input → bus call sites named in Q2 below.

Confirmed `CadGraph` lives in `EditorShell.cad_graph: Option<CadGraph>` and is mutated only via `CadGraph::begin_operation`/`graph_mut`/`commit`/`rollback`/`restore_to`, never through the bus.

+
+### Audit-root coverage (each MUST be read-only inspected per Acceptance Criteria)
+
+| Root | Inspected? | Evidence |
+| --- | --- | --- |

+| `crates/editor-shell/**` | yes | Read `src/{world.rs,pick_path.rs,audit.rs,snapshot.rs,lifecycle/commands.rs}` in full; read `src/lifecycle/mod.rs` 1600–1745; grep over `src/` for all bus identifiers + CadGraph identifiers; Cargo.toml. |
+| `editor/rge-editor/**` | yes | Grepped `src/main.rs` for `CommandBus|submit_action|command_bus|set_time_scale|EditorShell|handle_key_command`; only `EditorShell` constructor / wrapper references — zero direct bus references. |
+| `crates/editor-state/**` | yes | Read `src/lib.rs` + `src/active_tool.rs`; enumerated other modules (`selection`, `hover`, `face_selection`, `inspector_snapshot`, `modal_state`, `drag_drop`) via `rg --files`. |
+| `crates/cad-core/**` | yes | Read `src/checkpoints/mod.rs` in full; enumerated modules (operators, topology, graph, tessellation, topo_lineage, checkpoints/participate) via `rg --files`. |
+| `crates/cad-projection/**` | yes | Read `src/lib.rs` head + `Cargo.toml`; enumerated modules (`picking`, `projection_*`, `render_adapter`, `plugin_adapter`) via `rg --files`. |
+| `kernel/ecs/**` | yes | Read `src/lib.rs` head; enumerated modules (`world`, `snapshot`, `commands`, `participate`, `change_detection`, …) via `rg --files`. |
+
+## Halt-Check Outcomes (mandatory two checks)
+
+- **HC1 — World-only Action surface.** PASS (i.e., world-only is still in effect; no halt). Evidence: `crates/editor-actions/src/action.rs` defines `fn apply(&self, world: &mut World)` and `fn revert(&self, world: &mut World)` with `use rge_kernel_ecs::{EntityId, World};`. `crates/editor-actions/src/bus.rs` defines `pub fn submit(&mut self, action: Box<dyn Action>, world: &mut World) -> Result<(), BusError>` with `use rge_kernel_ecs::World;`. No editor-wide context type appears in the bus or trait signatures.
+- **HC2 — Production editor user path already calls `CommandBus::submit`.** PASS (i.e., no such path exists; no halt). Evidence:
+  - `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs` contains zero matches for `CommandBus`, `command_bus`, `submit_action`, or `set_time_scale`. The binary constructs an `EditorShell` and delegates lifecycle to it but does not itself reach the bus.
+  - In `crates/editor-shell/src/`, the only call sites that touch the bus are: the `Self::submit_action` / `Self::undo_command` / `Self::redo_command` / `Self::mark_saved_command` / `Self::command_bus` thin wrappers in `lifecycle/commands.rs`; the `EditorShell::set_time_scale` method in the same file; and the keyboard branch of `EditorShell::window_event` in `lifecycle/mod.rs:1700–1722`.
+  - The keyboard branch invokes **only** `handle_key_command(cmd)` which routes to `undo_command` / `redo_command` / `mark_saved_command` — i.e., to `CommandBus::{undo, redo, mark_saved}`, **never** to `CommandBus::submit`.
+  - `set_time_scale` is the only production wrapper that reaches `CommandBus::submit` (via `submit_action`), but it has **no production caller**. Grep of `set_time_scale` returns matches only in:
+    - `crates/editor-shell/tests/{time_scale_test.rs,inspector_snapshot_smoke.rs}` (integration tests),
+    - `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs` (unit tests, `#[cfg(test)]`),
+    - and the `lifecycle/{commands,mod}.rs` definition site / doc comments. No inspector UI, toolbar, or other production user-input branch reaches it.
+  - Therefore no production editor user path reaches `CommandBus::submit` directly or transitively. The keyboard `Ctrl+Z`/`Ctrl+Y`/`Ctrl+S` path reaches the bus's `undo`/`redo`/`mark_saved`, which the TASK packet halt-check specifically excludes (it names `submit`, including via wrappers such as `submit_action`).
+
+## 5-Question CommandBus Preflight Answer Block
+
+### Q1. Current Contract
+
+**CommandBus (`crates/editor-actions/src/bus.rs`).** `pub struct CommandBus { stack: UndoStack, coalesce: CoalesceWindow, ledger: AuditLedger, diagnostics: DiagnosticAggregator }`. The submit signature is `pub fn submit(&mut self, action: Box<dyn Action>, world: &mut World) -> Result<(), BusError>` with `use rge_kernel_ecs::World;` — it still takes a mutable kernel ECS `World` as the sole mutation target. Undo / redo also take `&mut World`. The bus owns its own audit ledger (`rge_kernel_audit_ledger::AuditLedger`) and applies, projects, and pushes in lockstep on each submit. Coalescing within a 500 ms window collapses same-`ActionId` submits without growing the stack.
+
+**Action trait (`crates/editor-actions/src/action.rs`).** `pub trait Action: Send + Sync + 'static` with `fn apply(&self, world: &mut World) -> Result<(), ActionResult>` and `fn revert(&self, world: &mut World) -> Result<(), ActionResult>`, plus optional `merge(&mut self, next: &dyn Action) -> MergeOutcome` and `payload(&self) -> Vec<u8>`. Both apply and revert are still `World`-only — they operate on `&mut rge_kernel_ecs::World`, not any editor-wide context.
+
+**Ownership.** The bus is owned by `EditorShell` (`crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:507`: `command_bus: CommandBus`) and constructed in every `EditorShell` builder (lines 609, 693, 941). Reach is through the thin shell-side surface in `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs:247–327` (`submit_action`, `undo_command`, `redo_command`, `mark_saved_command`, `command_bus`, `handle_key_command`). Every bus call passes `self.world.kernel_mut()` — i.e., the inner `rge_kernel_ecs::World` — so the wrapper `editor_shell::world::World` (which additionally holds blob storage) is never handed to the bus.
+
+**Existing in-workspace `Action` impls.** Only two production impls today: `editor-shell::lifecycle::commands::SetTimeScale` (the first non-test impl), which mutates a `TimeScale` resource via `World::insert_resource`. Everything else (`InsertVal`, `ReplaceVal`, `InsertMarker`) is `#[cfg(test)]` inside the `editor-actions` crate.
+
+**Production user path reaching `CommandBus::submit`?** **No.** See the HC2 outcome above. The only production code that reaches `CommandBus::submit` is `EditorShell::set_time_scale` → `submit_action` → `CommandBus::submit`, and `set_time_scale` itself has no production caller — all callers are `tests/` or `#[cfg(test)]`. The production keyboard branch in `EditorShell::window_event` reaches `CommandBus::{undo, redo, mark_saved}` for `Ctrl+Z`/`Y`/`S`, never `submit`. Halt-check HC2 not triggered.
+
+**Production user path that reaches the bus at all (non-submit).** `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:1676–1723` — the `WindowEvent::KeyboardInput` branch in `EditorShell::window_event` gates on `!egui_consumed`, calls `rge_input::translate_keyboard`, then `EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press(key, ctrl, shift)` and dispatches to `self.handle_key_command(cmd)`. That handler (`lifecycle/commands.rs:307–327`) routes to `undo_command` / `redo_command` / `mark_saved_command` — i.e., `CommandBus::undo` / `redo` / `mark_saved`. No production user input reaches `submit`.
+
+### Q2. First Input Paths
+
+Candidates for the **first** production editor user-action paths that would route mutations through the CommandBus, separated by category:
+
+**Production editor user-input paths (real winit events under `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`).**
+
+- **Keyboard `Ctrl+Z` / `Ctrl+Y` / `Ctrl+S`** (`WindowEvent::KeyboardInput`, `lifecycle/mod.rs:1676–1723`) — **already bus-wired** to `undo` / `redo` / `mark_saved`. Not a candidate for the *first* `submit` wiring (already navigates the bus, doesn't create entries).
+- **Mouse left-click face pick** (`WindowEvent::MouseInput`, `lifecycle/mod.rs:1641–1667` → `handle_left_click()` in `crates/editor-shell/src/pick_path.rs:58–121`). Today it writes directly to `self.coord.face_selection` (an `editor-state::FaceSelectionSet`) and rebuilds `self.highlight_index_buffer`. This is a user action with selection-state mutation; it would be the **first realistic candidate** for a bus-routed "set face selection" Action — but the mutation target is editor-coordination state, not `rge_kernel_ecs::World`, so the current `Action::apply(&mut World)` contract cannot represent it without context widening or an adapter.
+- **Cursor-move tracking** (`WindowEvent::CursorMoved`, `lifecycle/mod.rs:1634–1640`) — pure event-loop bookkeeping (`self.cursor_pos = ...`); not a candidate (no user-visible mutation, no undo semantics).
+- **Modifier tracking** (`WindowEvent::ModifiersChanged`, `lifecycle/mod.rs:1668–1675`) — bookkeeping; not a candidate.
+- **`KeyR` plain — asset hot-reload** (`lifecycle/mod.rs:1710–1721`, `handle_asset_reload`) — intentionally not bus-routed per `lifecycle/asset_reload.rs` doc comment ("Companion to commands.rs (CommandBus-bound) and playback.rs"). Lifecycle/devloop concern; not a candidate.
+- **`EditorPlaybackCommand` (`Space` / `Escape`)** (dispatched from the same keyboard branch via `self.handle_playback_command`, implemented in `lifecycle/playback.rs`). `playback.rs:5` states "No CommandBus involvement; no new ECS state; no toolbar UI." Intentional non-goal for the bus; not a candidate.
+
+**Production but indirect (shell methods that already reach `CommandBus::submit`, no production caller yet).**
+
+- **`EditorShell::set_time_scale(value: f32)`** (`lifecycle/commands.rs:348–382`). Already constructs `SetTimeScale { from, to }` and calls `self.submit_action(...)`. Zero production callers — only tests (`tests/time_scale_test.rs`, `tests/inspector_snapshot_smoke.rs`, `src/lifecycle/tests.rs`). The natural production caller is an egui inspector slider in the eventual inspector UI (`crates/editor-shell/src/inspector.rs` exists in the file listing but does not call `set_time_scale` today; not inspected here beyond its presence). This would be a visible-command wiring candidate, not a contract-shape candidate.
+
+**Non-candidates explicitly excluded from "first production paths".**
+
+- All `crates/editor-shell/tests/*.rs` files (e.g. `keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs`, `time_scale_test.rs`, `inspector_snapshot_smoke.rs`, `editor_coord_face_selection.rs`) — tests, not production.
+- `crates/editor-actions/tests/*.rs` and unit tests in `bus.rs` / `action.rs` — `InsertVal`, `ReplaceVal`, `InsertMarker` are `#[cfg(test)]` stubs.
+- `EditorShell::tick_game_systems` (legacy blob-storage advance, `world.rs:207`) — non-user lifecycle; intrinsic to the tick, not a user action.
+- `EditorShell::handle_asset_reload`, PIE Play/Pause/Stop/Step/FrameStep — non-user-content lifecycle (audit-only, snapshot-driven).
+- `egui` / `dock` widget reactions inside `egui_host` — render-only consumer of `InspectorSnapshot`; per the egui-host doc the reverse edge (host → shell) is forbidden, so user UI events route through winit branches first.
+
+**Summary.** The only production user-input candidate that today implies a *new* mutation rather than a bus navigation or a non-bus lifecycle is **mouse left-click face selection** (`handle_left_click`). It mutates `editor-state` (not `World`) and so is structurally incompatible with the present `Action::apply(&mut World)` contract — making it the load-bearing example for the Q3 comparison and the Q4 composition discussion.
+
+### Q3. Command-Context Shape
+
+Three approaches considered, each evaluated against the five axes named in the TASK packet.
+
+**A. Keep the existing World-only `Action` surface unchanged.**
+
+- *Dependency boundaries.* `editor-actions` keeps its current `Cargo.toml` (deps: `rge-kernel-ecs`, `rge-kernel-audit-ledger`, `rge-kernel-diagnostics`, plus `serde`, `thiserror`, `tracing`). No cross-tier dep changes. Editor-actions stays kernel-adjacent. Cleanest from the dependency-graph perspective; no `forbidden-dep` lint surface to renegotiate.
+- *Ownership authority.* `World` is the sole mutation surface the bus controls. `CadGraph` (in `cad-core`) and `editor-state` coordination structs remain outside the bus's authority. Mutations to them happen out-of-band (e.g. `handle_left_click` writes `self.coord.face_selection` directly; `CadGraph` mutations require `begin_operation`/`graph_mut`/`commit` against `EditorShell.cad_graph`).
+- *Audit / undo impact.* Bus audit-ledger (`rge_kernel_audit_ledger::AuditLedger` inside `CommandBus`) records one `EventKind::Action` per submit + truncation on redo-tail trim. `UndoStack` cursor moves in lockstep. For any user action whose effect is **not** representable as a `World`-component / `World`-resource mutation, the action either cannot be undone via the bus at all, or it must encode the side-effect as a `World` mutation (e.g. mirror the editor-state into typed components). The CAD checkpoint history (`CadGraph::CheckpointHistory`) remains a *separate* undo timeline; the bus cannot drive `restore_to`.
+- *Render invalidation impact.* `CadProjection::tick` is head-advanced-driven (`crates/cad-projection/src/lib.rs:36–52`): when `CadGraph::head()` moves, all known entities are marked dirty next tick. Since World-only Actions never advance `CadGraph::head`, this approach does not change render-invalidation semantics. Selection-overlay rebuild (`rebuild_highlight_overlay` in `pick_path.rs`) is also not bus-driven today; it remains a direct call site.
+- *Migration risk.* Lowest. No existing impls or tests need to change. Future commands that fit the contract (e.g. `set_time_scale`, future component edits) can be added incrementally. Risk is *content* rather than *contract*: many editor commands (CAD ops, selection, modal-state transitions) will be unable to use the bus at all, and so will accumulate parallel mutation paths.
+
+**B. Widen `Action` to an editor command context.**
+
+- *Dependency boundaries.* `editor-actions` would gain new mutation-target deps. To represent CAD-graph mutations (begin/commit/rollback against `CadGraph`) it would need a dep on `rge-cad-core`. To represent editor-state coordination mutations it would need a dep on `rge-editor-state`. Both are currently downstream of editor-actions in the dep graph (`editor-shell → {editor-actions, editor-state, cad-core, …}`); pulling `cad-core` and `editor-state` *into* `editor-actions` reshapes the crate's tier — it becomes a Tier-2 editor-domain coordinator instead of a thin kernel-adjacent mediation layer. The `forbidden-dep` architecture lint would need re-evaluation: `editor-actions` is not currently listed as a renderer or as `editor-ui`, so a tier-shift might not trip the existing rules directly, but it does invert the spirit of the doc comment ("the single mediation layer for editor mutations into runtime state").
+- *Ownership authority.* The bus becomes the single mediator for all editor mutation surfaces: `World`, `CadGraph` (with checkpoint history), `editor-state`, and (optionally) projection/audit. Authority is concentrated; out-of-band mutations become illegal.
+- *Audit / undo impact.* One unified undo timeline. Audit ledger and `CadGraph::CheckpointHistory` can co-cursor with the `UndoStack`. Eliminates the dual-ledger concern documented in `lifecycle/commands.rs:33–41`. But requires every existing impl (`SetTimeScale` and the test stubs in `editor-actions/src/{action.rs,bus.rs}`) to migrate to the widened signature; every test in `crates/editor-actions/tests/*.rs` and `crates/editor-shell/tests/{time_scale_test.rs,keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs,inspector_snapshot_smoke.rs}` would need re-shaping.
+- *Render invalidation impact.* Improved: CAD-graph commits now travel through the bus, so the bus can be a witness for "head advanced" events and could (in a later dispatch) call `projection.tick` after each commit. Selection-overlay rebuild could similarly hook in deterministically.
+- *Migration risk.* Highest. Touches a load-bearing trait, every existing impl, every test, and the editor-actions dependency graph. The crate's tier may need to change. Per `AGENTS.md` and the `editor-state-ownership` lint pattern documented in `editor-state/src/lib.rs`, expanding the mediation surface raises architectural sign-off questions that go beyond a single dispatch.
+
+**C. Adapter layer around the existing bus.**
+
+- *Dependency boundaries.* `editor-actions` keeps its current shape unchanged. New adapter types live in `editor-shell` (or a new `editor-shell::commands` submodule, alongside the existing `lifecycle/commands.rs`). Each "compound" editor command becomes a *wrapper function* that: captures pre-state on `CadGraph` / `editor-state` outside the `Action`; calls `EditorShell::submit_action` for the `World`-side effect; manually commits (or rolls back) the `CadGraph` operation; and records its own audit event on the editor-shell's `audit.rs` ring buffer (which is what `set_time_scale` already does in `lifecycle/commands.rs:381` via the dual-ledger pattern).
+- *Ownership authority.* Bus authority is unchanged (`&mut World` only). The adapter holds composite authority — but only for one command at a time, at the call site. Different adapters can pick different compositions, which means semantics are per-adapter rather than per-trait. Risk: the bus's `is_dirty` / cursor only reflects the World mutation, not the CAD or coord side, so `Ctrl+Z` might leave the CAD graph or editor-state inconsistent unless the adapter author manually pairs an inverse CAD `restore_to` with each bus `undo`.
+- *Audit / undo impact.* Dual (or triple) undo timelines persist. The bus's audit ledger records the World half of each compound command; the editor-shell `AuditEvent` ring records the editor-side half; `CadGraph::CheckpointHistory` records the CAD half. Replays must compose all three. The `set_time_scale` impl already demonstrates this dual-ledger pattern (`lifecycle/commands.rs:33–41`) and explicitly defers consolidation ("Ledger consolidation is a separate future dispatch").
+- *Render invalidation impact.* Same as A unless the adapter pulls in `projection.tick` calls explicitly. Selection-overlay rebuild remains a direct call.
+- *Migration risk.* Medium. No trait or `editor-actions` changes; no test rewrites. But every new compound command must hand-author its undo/audit pairing, which is precisely the divergence risk the original Command Bus design tried to eliminate (`bus.rs:76` — "the single mediation layer"). Long-term entropy is higher.
+
+**Comparison summary.**
+
+| Axis | A. World-only | B. Widened context | C. Adapter layer |
+| --- | --- | --- | --- |
+| Dep boundaries | unchanged | editor-actions gains cad-core + editor-state deps, tier shifts | unchanged |
+| Ownership authority | bus owns World only | bus owns World + CadGraph + editor-state | bus owns World only; adapters own the rest |
+| Audit / undo | one bus ledger + UndoStack; CAD + editor-state out-of-band | one unified timeline | dual/triple ledgers; per-adapter pairing |
+| Render invalidation | unaffected; CAD head doesn't move | bus can drive `projection.tick` | same as A unless adapter calls it |
+| Migration risk | lowest | highest (every impl + tests + dep graph + tier sign-off) | medium; divergence risk grows over time |
+
+No recommendation is made in Q3 — Q5 names the smallest next step.
+
+### Q4. Undo/Audit/Render Composition
+
+For a future visible editor command (e.g. "click face to add it to selection", "drag transform gizmo to translate an entity", "extrude selected face by Δh") the composition needs to land in five places:
+
+1. **`rge_kernel_ecs::World` mutation** (entity components / resources) — e.g., a `Transform` component edit, or a `TimeScale` resource update. *Already composes through the current bus.* `Action::apply(&mut World)` covers this directly; `SetTimeScale` demonstrates end-to-end (apply, revert via `insert_resource`, payload-driven coalesce).
+2. **CAD operator-graph mutation** (`cad_core::CadGraph` via `begin_operation` → `graph_mut` → `commit`/`rollback`, or `restore_to`). *Does not compose through the current bus.* The bus has no handle to `EditorShell.cad_graph`; `Action::apply(&mut World)` cannot reach it. This requires either a widened context (approach B) or a hand-wired adapter (approach C). Without one of those, CAD commits have an independent undo timeline (`CheckpointHistory`), and `Ctrl+Z` against the bus reverts only the `World` half.
+3. **Editor coordination state mutation** (`editor-state` — `Selection`, `Hover`, `ActiveTool`, `FaceSelection`, etc., owned at `EditorShell.coord`). *Does not compose through the current bus.* Same reason as (2): `coord` is not a `World`. `pick_path.rs::handle_left_click` mutates `self.coord.face_selection` directly today; nothing about that path knows of the bus. Same fix space as (2).
+4. **Audit-ledger projection.** Two ledgers exist:
+   - The bus's internal `rge_kernel_audit_ledger::AuditLedger` records `EventKind::Action` on each submit (`bus.rs:187`). *Already composes for every World-only submit.*
+   - The editor-shell `audit::AuditLedger` (a ring buffer of `AuditEvent { PlayPressed, …, TimeScaleChanged, SnapshotCaptured, … }`) records lifecycle and editor-side events. *Does not compose through the bus*; each call site appends directly (`set_time_scale` does both: bus submit + `self.audit.record(AuditEvent::TimeScaleChanged{from,to})`). This dual-ledger is an explicit, locked design decision per `lifecycle/commands.rs:33–41`; consolidation is deferred.
+5. **Snapshots, dirty state, render refresh.**
+   - **`CommandBus::is_dirty()`** already drives the unsaved-changes flag (cursor-vs-save-mark on `UndoStack`); `mark_saved_command` clears it. *Composes for every World-only submit*; for editor-state-only or CAD-only mutations not routed through the bus, `is_dirty` does not flip — so the "any unsaved change" indicator may underreport unless those side mutations are bus-routed (approach B) or the adapter explicitly does a bus submit (approach C).
+   - **PIE `WorldSnapshot`** captures and restores the kernel ECS world only (`crates/editor-shell/src/snapshot.rs`); editor-state intentionally persists across Play/Stop (per the `editor-state` doc-comment). Snapshots are orthogonal to the bus today and need no composition change for the bus integration itself.
+   - **`CadProjection::tick`** is head-advance-driven (`cad-projection/src/lib.rs:36–52`). After a CAD-graph commit it re-projects dirty entities and refreshes `BRepHandle` payloads. **Composes correctly via the current loop** as long as `CadGraph::commit` happens at all; the open question is *who* makes the commit (bus, adapter, or direct call). Selection-overlay (`rebuild_highlight_overlay` in `pick_path.rs`) is direct-called from `handle_left_click`; if face-selection becomes bus-routed in the future, the overlay rebuild needs to fire from the bus-side (or from an adapter), not from the click handler.
+   - **`InspectorSnapshot`** (`crates/editor-state/src/inspector_snapshot.rs`) is read-only observation. Composes regardless of approach.
+
+**Already composes through the current bus.** World-only mutations (component / resource edits, `SetTimeScale`-style); bus's internal audit ledger; `UndoStack` cursor + save-mark; `is_dirty` for World-only mutations.
+
+**Requires context design or adapter authority before it composes.** CAD-graph mutation (begin/commit/restore), editor-state coordination mutation, the editor-shell side audit ledger (whether to consolidate or keep dual-ledger), and the `is_dirty` flag's coverage of non-World mutations. Render invalidation composes structurally either way but needs a clear authority for *when* `CadProjection::tick` runs after a bus-routed CAD commit.
+
+### Q5. Smallest Follow-Up
+
+**NEEDS_HUMAN.**
+
+The choice between the three approaches in Q3 is an architecture-tier decision that an executor cannot resolve from read-only code evidence alone. Specifically:
+
+- Approach **A** (World-only) is technically immediate but *architecturally insufficient* for the load-bearing first input path identified in Q2 (left-click face selection mutates editor-state, not World) and for any future CAD-graph editor command (which must travel through `CadGraph::begin_operation`/`commit`).
+- Approach **B** (widened editor context) requires promoting `editor-actions` from a kernel-adjacent mediation layer to a Tier-2 editor-domain coordinator (adding `rge-cad-core` and `rge-editor-state` to `crates/editor-actions/Cargo.toml`). This inverts the existing crate doc comment ("the single mediation layer for editor mutations into runtime state" — currently kernel-adjacent) and is a tier-graph reshape that affects the `forbidden-dep` lint posture across the workspace.
+- Approach **C** (adapter layer) preserves the existing tier but accepts permanent dual-ledger + multiple undo timelines, contradicting the original "single mediation layer" intent and pushing per-command divergence risk onto every future adapter author.
+
+The smallest **human** decision needed is: **pick the editor command-context shape — World-only Action, widened editor-context Action, or adapter-layer — with explicit sign-off on whether `editor-actions` may shift to a Tier-2 coordinator (B), or whether dual-ledger and parallel undo timelines are acceptable as a permanent posture (C), or whether the editor accepts that user actions touching CAD or editor-state will not be undoable via the bus (A).**
+
+This decision is also the prerequisite for a follow-up implementation dispatch. Without it, any "smallest" implementation dispatch would have to either silently commit to one approach (risking later rework) or combine context design with visible-command wiring in a single dispatch (which the TASK packet's Halt Conditions and Acceptance Criteria explicitly forbid). Q5 must not combine command-context design and visible command wiring; therefore the audit defers the design decision to a human arbiter rather than naming a half-step that either prejudges B/C or prematurely wires a command under A.
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+None — execution stayed strictly within the Task Packet scope. No source, tests, docs, manifests, scripts, schemas, status files, workflows, lint configs, existing packets, or non-EXEC handoff packets were edited or added; only this single EXEC report was created. No cargo / test / formatter / architecture-lint / benchmark / doctest / `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` / source-generation / docs-generation command was run.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+- The Q5 NEEDS_HUMAN framing rests on the tier-classification question for `editor-actions`. If the project already has a frozen ADR or `forbidden-dep` rule that resolves the tier question for `editor-actions` (currently neither `is_game_domain` nor `is_renderer_crate` nor `editor-ui` in the existing rules as referenced in `crates/cad-projection/Cargo.toml:34–39`), the human arbiter may be able to short-circuit the decision toward A, B, or C without a full architecture round. The Executor did not inspect `crates/tool-architecture-lints/**` or workspace-level ADR files because they are outside the TASK packet's mandatory audit roots and reading them was not required to answer Q1–Q4.
+- The Executor classified `EditorShell::set_time_scale` as "no production caller" based on grep of `crates/editor-shell` and `editor/rge-editor` for the literal `set_time_scale`. If a production caller exists outside those two roots (e.g. in a `crates/editor-ui` or future `inspector` UI crate not yet present in this branch), please verify; this audit did not enumerate every workspace crate.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files: clean.
+- Untracked items: `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-108_TASK_2026-05-23_09-59-46+0300.md` and `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-108_TASK_2026-05-23_09-59-46+0300.meta.json` (pre-existing TASK packet, present before the audit and documented as such); `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-108_EXEC_2026-05-23_10-08-36+0300.md` (this report; the only Executor-created change). The EXEC sidecar will be created by `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize`.
+- Branch: `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-108`.

+

+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-108
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-23_09-59-46+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/editor-actions/**
+- crates/editor-shell/**
+- editor/rge-editor/**
+- crates/editor-state/**
+- crates/cad-core/**
+- crates/cad-projection/**
+- kernel/ecs/**
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-108_EXEC_*.md
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Perform GitHub issue #108 as a read-only CommandBus editor user-action integration preflight. The Executor must inspect the current CommandBus contract and the editor user-input/action/render/audit surfaces across the required roots, compare viable integration approaches, and produce one evidence-backed EXEC report with the exact five-question CommandBus preflight answer block. This dispatch must not implement wiring, change command-context design, or edit any repository file other than the required execution report.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- None. This is an audit-only dispatch.
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- `crates/editor-actions/**`
+- `crates/editor-shell/**`
+- `editor/rge-editor/**`
+- `crates/editor-state/**`
+- `crates/cad-core/**`
+- `crates/cad-projection/**`
+- `kernel/ecs/**`
+- Any source file.
+- Any test file.
+- Any documentation file.
+- `Cargo.toml`
+- `Cargo.lock`
+- Any workspace manifest, dependency declaration, feature flag, or Cargo metadata file.
+- `.github/workflows/**`
+- Any script, schema, status file, lint configuration, `.gitignore`, generated artifact, persisted log, or repository metadata file.
+- Any existing handoff packet, including this TASK packet.
+- Any handoff packet other than the single required `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-108_EXEC_*.md` execution report.
+
+### MAY add new files
+- Exactly one execution report matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-108_EXEC_*.md`.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- New source files, tests, docs, fixtures, generated artifacts, persisted logs, screenshots, Cargo entries, dependency files, crates, architecture lints, schemas, scripts, workflow files, TASK packets, REVIEW packets, CORRECT packets, or CLOSEOUT packets.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- One `EXECUTION_REPORT` packet under `ai_handoffs/` and no other repository changes.
+- A command log in the EXEC report listing every local read/search/status command used for the audit, including any fallback used because `rg` is unavailable.
+- Mandatory read-only inspection of all required audit roots: `crates/editor-actions/**`, `crates/editor-shell/**`, `editor/rge-editor/**`, `crates/editor-state/**`, `crates/cad-core/**`, `crates/cad-projection/**`, and `kernel/ecs/**`.
+- A concise evidence inventory of current CommandBus contract files, first editor user-input paths, command-context candidates, undo/audit behavior, render/snapshot invalidation points, and CAD/projection/world boundaries.
+- The exact answer section shown below, with all five headings present in this order:
+
+```markdown
+## 5-Question CommandBus Preflight Answer Block
+
+### Q1. Current Contract
+
+### Q2. First Input Paths
+
+### Q3. Command-Context Shape
+
+### Q4. Undo/Audit/Render Composition
+
+### Q5. Smallest Follow-Up
+```
+
+- Q1 must state the current CommandBus and `Action` contract, including whether `Action::apply` and `Action::revert` are still `World`-only, whether `CommandBus::submit` still takes a mutable ECS `World`, where the bus is owned, and whether any production editor user path already reaches `CommandBus::submit` directly or transitively.
+- Q2 must identify the first editor user-action input paths that would be candidates for CommandBus integration, distinguishing production editor paths from tests, stubs, inactive helpers, render-only code, and non-user lifecycle code.
+- Q3 must compare all three approaches before recommending anything: keeping the existing World-only `Action` surface, widening `Action` to an editor command context, and using an adapter-layer approach around the existing bus. The comparison must cover dependency boundaries, ownership authority, audit/undo impact, render invalidation impact, and migration risk.
+- Q4 must describe how undo, audit-ledger projection, snapshots, dirty state, CAD graph changes, editor-state changes, and render refresh should compose for a future visible editor command. It must identify which pieces can already compose through the current bus and which pieces would require context design or adapter authority.
+- Q5 must name exactly one smallest follow-up dispatch with allowed files, must-not-touch surfaces, verification gates, and halt conditions, or say `NEEDS_HUMAN` and name the smallest human decision needed. Q5 must not combine command-context design and visible command wiring into one follow-up dispatch.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The dispatch is audit-only. No source, tests, docs, manifests, lockfiles, workflows, scripts, schemas, status files, existing packets, or non-EXEC handoff packets are edited or added.
+- Exactly one new EXEC packet is produced for this dispatch.
+- The EXEC packet explicitly states this was a read-only CommandBus editor user-action integration preflight and that no local cargo commands, tests, formatters, architecture lints, or `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` were run.
+- The EXEC packet documents read-only inspection of every mandatory audit root: `crates/editor-actions/**`, `crates/editor-shell/**`, `editor/rge-editor/**`, `crates/editor-state/**`, `crates/cad-core/**`, `crates/cad-projection/**`, and `kernel/ecs/**`.
+- The EXEC packet contains the exact `## 5-Question CommandBus Preflight Answer Block` heading and the exact Q1-Q5 subheadings listed in `Deliverables`.
+- Q1 answers the current CommandBus contract from code evidence, including the two halt checks: production editor user path already reaches `CommandBus::submit`, and CommandBus no longer exposes a World-only `Action` surface.
+- Q2 answers first input paths from code evidence and clearly separates real editor user actions from tests or non-user paths.
+- Q3 compares World-only `Action`, widened editor-context `Action`, and adapter-layer approaches before Q5 names a follow-up.
+- Q4 covers undo/audit/render composition without proposing implementation edits.
+- Q5 names exactly one smallest follow-up or `NEEDS_HUMAN`; it must not combine command-context design and visible command wiring into one follow-up dispatch.
+- If a production editor user path already calls `CommandBus::submit` directly or transitively, the Executor halts and writes the EXEC report with header `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, footer `HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, and `NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER`.
+- If `CommandBus` no longer has a World-only `Action` surface, the Executor halts and writes the EXEC report with header `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, footer `HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, and `NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER`.
+- If all audit questions are answered without a halt condition, the EXEC report footer contains exactly one line-anchored `HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE` marker and routes `NEXT_ROLE: REVIEWER_AI`.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- This dispatch is a read-only preflight. It must not implement CommandBus wiring, change action traits, add command context, add adapters, migrate commands, alter render invalidation, or update tests.
+- Do not run local cargo commands, tests, formatters, architecture lints, benchmarks, doctests, or `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`.
+- Do not run `cargo check`, `cargo build`, `cargo test`, `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy`, `cargo run -p rge-tool-architecture-lints`, or any equivalent command.
+- Do not edit source, tests, docs, `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, workflows, scripts, schemas, status files, lint files, existing packets, or any non-EXEC file.
+- Do not create a follow-up TASK packet, REVIEW packet, CORRECT packet, CLOSEOUT packet, issue note, design doc, ADR, code comment, commit, branch, PR, or GitHub issue comment.
+- Do not broaden the audit into unrelated editor UI, egui host, asset-store, CI, or architecture-lint work.
+- Do not combine command-context design and visible command wiring into one recommended follow-up.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document each applicable gate in the `EXECUTION_REPORT`. These gates are read-only only:
+
+- Run `git status --short --untracked-files=no` before the audit. Document any pre-existing tracked changes. If tracked changes inside the audit surfaces make read-only conclusions unreliable, halt with `NEEDS_HUMAN`; do not clean or revert them.
+- Inventory files under all mandatory audit roots. Use `rg --files crates/editor-actions crates/editor-shell editor/rge-editor crates/editor-state crates/cad-core crates/cad-projection kernel/ecs` if `rg` is available; otherwise use an equivalent PowerShell recursive file listing.
+- Confirm each mandatory audit root exists and was inspected: `crates/editor-actions/**`, `crates/editor-shell/**`, `editor/rge-editor/**`, `crates/editor-state/**`, `crates/cad-core/**`, `crates/cad-projection/**`, and `kernel/ecs/**`.
+- Search the mandatory audit roots for CommandBus and action-contract evidence. Suggested patterns include `CommandBus`, `CommandBus::submit`, `submit_action`, `undo_command`, `redo_command`, `command_bus`, `trait Action`, `impl Action`, `Action::apply`, `Action::revert`, `World`, `AuditLedger`, `UndoStack`, `is_dirty`, `mark_saved`, and `ledger`.
+- Search the mandatory audit roots for first editor user-action paths and event-routing evidence. Suggested patterns include `WindowEvent`, `KeyboardInput`, `MouseInput`, `CursorMoved`, `ElementState`, `KeyCode`, `translate_keyboard`, `handle_window_event`, `handle_event`, `submit_action`, `undo_command`, `redo_command`, `active_tool`, `selection`, `face_selection`, `pick`, `drag`, `click`, and `toolbar`.
+- Search the mandatory audit roots for command-context, CAD/projection, snapshot, and render-composition evidence. Suggested patterns include `CadGraph`, `checkpoint`, `transaction`, `rollback`, `restore_to`, `snapshot`, `PieSnapshot`, `serialize_snapshot`, `restore_from_snapshot`, `InspectorSnapshot`, `RenderInput`, `render_frame`, `invalidate`, `dirty`, `projection`, `CadProjection`, and `pick_face`.
+- Read the files necessary to answer Q1-Q5 from the mandatory audit roots, including the current `crates/editor-actions` CommandBus and `Action` files, current editor-shell lifecycle/input files, current editor-state coordination files, relevant CAD graph/checkpoint files, relevant cad-projection picking/render adapter files, and relevant ECS `World`/snapshot files.
+- Record every grep/search and read command used in the EXEC report.
+- Confirm the EXEC report contains the exact `## 5-Question CommandBus Preflight Answer Block` heading and exact Q1-Q5 subheadings listed in `Deliverables`.
+- Run `git status --short` after writing the EXEC packet. Expected result: no Executor-created changes outside the single new `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-108_EXEC_*.md` report, aside from any pre-existing changes documented before the audit.
+
+No local cargo build, cargo test, cargo fmt, cargo clippy, cargo-deny, benchmark, architecture-lint run, `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`, source generation, docs generation, or manual verification beyond read/search/status commands is allowed for this audit-only dispatch.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without editing existing files and request fresh guidance if any of the following occur:
+
+- A production editor user path already calls `CommandBus::submit` directly or transitively. For this task, production editor user path means code under `editor/rge-editor/src/**` or `crates/editor-shell/src/**` that handles real user input or visible editor commands and reaches `CommandBus::submit`, including through wrappers such as `submit_action`; tests, examples, comments, and inactive stubs do not count by themselves.
+- `CommandBus` no longer has a World-only `Action` surface. For this task, World-only means `Action::apply` and `Action::revert` operate on `&mut rge_kernel_ecs::World`, and `CommandBus::submit` applies an action using a mutable ECS `World` rather than an editor-wide context.
+- Answering Q1-Q5 requires editing source, tests, docs, manifests, workflows, scripts, schemas, status files, existing packets, or any file other than the single EXEC report.
+- A required audit root is missing or unreadable.
+- The audit cannot classify current contract, first input paths, command-context shape, undo/audit/render composition, or smallest follow-up from the allowed read-only evidence.
+- The smallest plausible follow-up would combine command-context design and visible command wiring in one dispatch.
+- The smallest follow-up cannot be bounded with explicit allowed files, must-not-touch surfaces, verification gates, and halt conditions.
+- Any local cargo command, test, formatter, architecture lint, benchmark, doctest, cargo-deny command, `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`, source generation, or docs generation appears necessary to answer the audit.
+- The Executor accidentally runs a prohibited command; if this happens, stop immediately and report `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+- The final working tree contains Executor-created changes outside the one new `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-108_EXEC_*.md` report.
+
+If a halt condition fires, the Executor must still write the single EXEC report if possible. The report should include the required five-question block; unanswered questions may say `HALTED:` with the concrete reason, and Q5 must say `NEEDS_HUMAN`. Use header `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, footer `HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, and `NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER` for the two mandatory halt checks or any architecture/product judgment requiring human arbitration. Use `STATUS: BLOCKED`, footer `HANDOFF_STATUS: BLOCKED`, and `NEXT_ROLE: PLANNER_AI` only for a narrow missing-context blocker that the Planner can resolve without human architecture judgment.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+The purpose is to decide the next safe CommandBus integration step before implementation. The audit must not assume that the existing World-only command surface is sufficient for CAD graph/editor-state/render-side user actions, and it must not jump directly to visible command wiring. The Executor must first prove the current contract, identify real input paths, compare World-only, widened-context, and adapter-layer designs, and then name one smallest follow-up or `NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+
+Under the orchestrator protocol, no `_REVIEW_` or `_CLOSEOUT_` packet is expected from this TASK. The only new packet produced by execution should be the `ISSUE-108_EXEC_*.md` report.
